stem: The movement of food in the alimentary canal is brought about by the smooth muscles in the body. Which of the following options has all the organs having smooth muscles?
options: Iris of the eye, bronchi of lungs, ureters
Heart, iris of the eye, bones

stem: The Minimum Needs Programme was introduced in India in the ______ year of the Fifth Five-Year Plan.
Which of the following quadratic equations has one of its roots as 4 − 2√5?
In March 2022, Indian badminton player ______ defeated Olympic champion Viktor Axelsen to enter the men's singles final of the German Open Tournament.

If '−' means '×', '×' means '÷', '÷' means '+', and '+' means '−', what will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation?
40 − 17 ÷ 3 × 25 + 8 = ?
Which of the following properties is NOT governed by the position of elements in the Periodic Table?
A train travelling at a speed of 45 km/h crosses a pole in 24 sec. What is the length of the train?

Which of the following is NOT a use of plaster of Paris?
(i) Manufacture of glass, soap and paper
(ii) Used in plastic for supporting fractured bones
(iii) Making toys, materials for decoration and for making surfaces smooth
(iv) Used in chemistry laboratories for sealing air gaps in apparatus where air tight arrangement is required
Which of the following statements is INCORRECT regarding the surgical methods of birth control?
options: They block gamete transport and prevent fertilisation
In males vas deferens and in females fallopian tube is blocked
They are not very effective methods
They are also called sterilisation procedures

stem: Amongst six friends P, Q, R, S, T and U, each has a different height. S is taller than only S can while shorter than P. P is not taller than R. Q is taller than P. U is taller than S but shorter than P but taller than Q. Who is the second tallest among all the friends?
The angle between thumb and middle finger in Fleming's left-hand rule is:
If the simple interest on ₹4,300 at 15% per annum for 'a' years is ₹2,160, then find the value of 'a'.

In February 2022, scientists from Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) and ______ successfully demonstrated Quantum Key Distribution link between Prayagraj and Vindhyachal in Uttar Pradesh.
Find the area (in square units) of a triangle with vertices (3,4), (5,7) and (−3,6).

reason: Requires a Venn diagram with numbered regions to answer; not expressible in text.

stem: A trader gives 5% discount on the marked price of an article and gives 2 articles free for buying 14 articles and gains 19% on the entire transaction. Assuming a customer pays for 14 articles, the marked price of an article is what per cent more than its cost price?
What is the main difference observed between a slide of human cheek cells and a slide of onion peel when viewed under a compound microscope?
options: Presence of plastids in cheek cells
Presence of cell wall in onion peel cells
Presence of cell wall in human cheek cells
Presence of mitochondria in onion peel cells

stem: The sum of two consecutive multiples of 6 is 66. Find the smaller of these two multiples.

Simplify the given expression.
(13² − 5²)^(1/2) × 8^(2/3) ÷ (1/6)^(−3)
What is the formula of cyclohexane?
Which of the following organisms shows a parasitic nutritive strategy?

Generally, a Gram Panchayat is constituted for ______ year/s.
